# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node runtime as a parent image
FROM node:14

# Set working directory in the container
WORKDIR /usr/src/app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json to the work directory
COPY package*.json ./

# Install Node.js dependencies
RUN npm install

# Install system utilities
RUN apt-get update && \
    apt-get install -y curl && \
    apt-get install -y build-essential && \
    apt-get install -y vim && \
    apt-get install -y net-tools && \
    apt-get install -y git

# Bundle app source
COPY . .

# Bind the port that the app will run on
EXPOSE 8080

# Run the app
CMD [ "node", "index.js" ]
